references.snippet: - `packs/` is canonical in runtime packaging, activation, compatibility, and distribution-descriptor scope
- `data/packs/` remains authoritative within authored pack content and declaration scope, but stays transitional and residual-quarantined for any attempted single-root convergence
- `packs/` vs `data/packs/` is not a free equivalence
- `packs/` and `data/packs/` have scoped ownership and are not interchangeable

- Packs: `repo/packs/<pack_id>/<version>/pack.tlv` (single TLV per version). Version directories are 8-digit, zero-padded integers (e.g. `00000001`). The built-in base pack lives at `repo/packs/base/00000001/pack.tlv`.
- Unknown fields are ignored so packs/mods can extend their own metadata without breaking the engine.

- `--dev-allow-missing-content=0|1` allows empty content packs/mods in dev/test
- Content (packs/mods/assets): data-only and scripting atop Dominium; never calls OS APIs directly; interacts via defined Domino/Dominium interfaces.
- Policy: never silently break old saves/packs/mods. Prefer read-compatible migrations; hard failures must be explicit and documented.
- Installs, repairs, uninstalls, and imports products/packs/mods into `DOMINIUM_HOME` without destructive side effects.
